# SkyWatch

> A free web app that tells you whether tonight is worth going outside for
> stargazing, and exactly which area near you has the best conditions.

## What this project is

SkyWatch is a Python + Streamlit dashboard that combines real-time weather
data, astronomical calculations, and a curated database of 116 US dark-sky
locations to answer one question: is tonight a good night to go outside?

It is a non-commercial, open-source project. All data sources are free and
publicly available. No API keys are required to run it.

## What it does

- Calculates sunrise, sunset, moonrise, moonset, golden hour, civil twilight,
  astronomical twilight, and Milky Way visibility windows for any US location
- Scores sky quality (0–100) for the next 7 nights based on cloud cover and
  moon phase
- Recommends nearby dark-sky areas, national parks, and viewpoints ranked by
  tonight's actual forecast conditions
- Provides plain-English summaries with technical terms available in tooltips

## Tech stack

- Language: Python 3
- Framework: Streamlit
- Astronomy: astronomy-engine (all calculations run locally, no API)
- Weather: Open-Meteo API (free, no key required)
- Geocoding: Open-Meteo Geocoding API (free, no key required)
- Visualisation: Altair
- Timezone: timezonefinder
- Spot data: curated JSON — 116 locations across all 50 US states

## File structure

- app.py              — Main Streamlit dashboard
- astro_engine.py     — Sky calculation engine (SkyEngine class)
- spot_finder.py      — Spot discovery, distance ranking, plain-English output
- spots.json          — Curated US viewing locations with Bortle estimates
- logger.py           — Lightweight anonymised usage logging
- pages/monitoring.py — Internal usage monitoring dashboard
- requirements.txt    — Python dependencies

## Data sources

- astronomy-engine (MIT) — sun, moon, Milky Way, planets, meteor showers
- Open-Meteo (free, non-commercial) — hourly cloud cover forecasts
- DarkSky International — certified dark sky place names and locations
- NPS open data — national park coordinates
- USGS GNIS — named US places (via geocoding)
- Bortle scale estimates — manually curated per spot (not yet verified against VIIRS)

## Limitations LLMs should be aware of

- Bortle (sky darkness) ratings per spot are estimates, not verified satellite data
- Drive times shown are rough estimates (straight-line distance × circuity factor)
  not real routing
- Spot coordinates are area-level, not pinpoint — always verify access before visiting
- Weather forecasts are sourced from Open-Meteo and reflect forecast uncertainty
- Milky Way visibility is based on galactic center altitude > 10° — terrain and
  trees are not yet modelled
- The app covers US locations only

## How to run

```bash
git clone https://github.com/sidni5/skywatch.git
cd skywatch
python3 -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
streamlit run app.py
```

## Contact

GitHub: https://github.com/sidni5/skywatch
Live app: https://skywatch.streamlit.app
